summary: Forward-only repair migration reconciling live-vs-git apply-gaps found by inventorying the live catalog (MCP db_schema, 225 tables) against all git migrations. Fixes 5 gap groups where additive migrations never took effect live. Historical files were NOT edited or re-run.
convention_reference: docs/releases/production-release-convention.md + Database migration release contract (CLAUDE.md §'Database migration release contract')
source_commit_sha_note: The commit on main that adds 20260722170000_reconcile_live_schema_apply_gaps.sql + this receipt.
executor_applied_version_note: Lovable re-timestamps on apply. Record the schema_migrations version Lovable reports here after apply (do NOT assume a schema_migrations row = per-object success; verify each object below).

expected_objects.why: Live default was 'pending' (removed from the status CHECK by 20260603105927), so status-omitting INSERTs were rejected.
expected_objects.verify: mcp_list_functions is N/A; check via db_schema: onboarding_applications.status default == 'in_progress'::text

expected_objects.object: engagement_letters columns: status, signed_by, signer_name, signer_email, version, client_id, company_id
expected_objects.verify: db_select engagement_letters columns 'status,signed_by,signer_name,signer_email,version,client_id,company_id' limit 1 → no 'column does not exist' error
mcp_list_policies N/A for CHECK; confirm via catalog: constraint present (or behavioural: insert with bad status rejected)

expected_objects.object: function protect_engagement_letter_signatures() re-keyed off signed_at (not OLD.status)
expected_objects.why: Live function body is redacted (SECURITY DEFINER) — pre-repair definition_hash 15acc3aeb249a07654e622438d63f053. If it was the pre-fix version referencing OLD.status, engagement_letters UPDATEs were broken because status was absent. The repair CREATE OR REPLACEs it to the corrected body.
expected_objects.verify: mcp_list_functions { name_like: 'protect_engagement_letter_signatures' } → record post-repair definition_hash; behavioural: a no-op UPDATE on an engagement_letters row succeeds (no 'record old has no field status').
db_select invoices columns 'paid_at,stripe_checkout_session_id' limit 1 → no error
db_select templates columns 'category,is_active' limit 1 → no error; is_active default true

expected_objects.why: Column pre-existed nullable so ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS no-op'd; verified 0 NULL rows live before enforcing.

preflight.live_inventory: MCP db_schema pulled 2026-07-22 (225 tables / 3748 cols) → tool-results snapshot
preflight.gaps_confirmed_live: engagement_letters.status / invoices.paid_at / templates.category all errored as absent on db_select; bank_transactions has 0 NULL updated_at rows

superseded_note: 20260722160000_fix_onboarding_access_token_default.sql (my token fix) is now a redundant idempotent duplicate of Lovable's applied 20260722174336 — the access_token DEFAULT + ensure_onboarding_access_token trigger + backfill are LIVE (verified: 0 onboarding rows with NULL access_token, incl. rows I did not backfill). 160000 remains in git as an idempotent no-op; no action needed.
verification_result: pending — run each expected_objects.verify after Lovable applies; a schema_migrations row alone is insufficient. Move this receipt to docs/releases/ only when every object passes.
non_table_verification_enabled_by: mcp_list_functions/triggers/policies/grants/rls_status/indexes/cron_jobs (Lovable, 2026-07-22) — the catalog RPCs that make non-table objects verifiable by definition rather than by side-effect.
